channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Trump says top Islamic State leader was killed in Nigeria strike
item.dc:creator: Victoria Craw, Rachel Chason, Dan Lamothe
item.description: Trump said the operation killed Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, whom he described as "second in command of ISIS globally." Nigeria's president said the strike took place at a compound in the Lake Chad basin.

item.title: Trump’s China summit shows toll of a difficult year for the president
item.dc:creator: Michael Birnbaum, Isaac Arnsdorf
item.description: A year ago, Trump promised a new era during the first major foreign trip of his second term. On his recent visit to Beijing, the war with Iran and economic strain clouded his diplomacy.

item.title: U.S. sank $970 million in mission that left Haiti worse off. It’s trying again.
item.dc:creator: Widlore Mérancourt, Amanda Coletta
item.description: A larger multinational security force will have broader authority to combat rising gang violence than its predecessor but could face similar funding challenges.

item.title: Tumultuous, bloody week unfolds in Ukraine and Russia after brief ceasefire
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina, Anastacia Galouchka
item.description: A Russian airstrike on a Kyiv apartment complex that killed at least 24 people and a Ukrainian strike on residential buildings and an oil refinery in Ryazan, Russia, suggested no end is in sight to the war.

item.title: In northern Ukraine, it was boy vs. Russian drone. The boy won.
item.dc:creator: Steve Hendrix, Kostiantyn Khudov
item.description: A soldier taught a 12-year-old how to disable the fiber-optic drones that Russia has been using to hunt Ukrainian civilians in a campaign the U.N. has labeled a war crime.

item.title: In pageantry and politics, China summit yields Xi’s goal — equal footing with U.S.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Arnsdorf, Michael Birnbaum, Michelle Lee
item.description: The image of peer superpowers during President Trump’s visit displayed a dynamic that analysts say the Chinese have long sought and Americans had resisted.

item.title: Xi warns Trump that mishandling Taiwan could spark ‘conflicts’
item.dc:creator: Michael Birnbaum, Isaac Arnsdorf, Rebecca Tan
item.description: China’s leader made clear his top priority is the fate of the contested island and its U.S. military support, a striking move given President Donald Trump’s effort to mend ties and deliver trade deals.
